Title: CP Admin API Access
Description: Verify Kong CP Admin API is accessible and secured
Test steps:
1. From workstation, hit CP Admin API endpoint
2. Verify authentication is required
3. Verify only internal network can reach CP
Expected results:
Admin API requires authentication
CP not exposed externally

Title: DP Proxy traffic flow
Description: Verify DP proxies traffic from workstation to demo server
Test steps:
1. Deploy a service+route on Kong pointing to demo server
2. From workstation, send request via DP proxy port
3. Confirm request reaches demo server
Expected results:
Traffic flows: Workstation -> Kong DP -> Demo Server
Response returned to workstation

Title: PostgreSQL connectivity
Description: Verify CP connects to PostgreSQL and persists config
Test steps:
1. Check Kong CP logs for DB connection
2. Create a route via Admin API
3. Restart Kong CP pod
4. Verify route still exists after restart
Expected results: Config persists in PostgreSQL across restarts

Title: API Key authentication
Description: Verify Kong enforces API key on configured routes
Test steps:
1. Enable key-auth plugin on a test route
2. Create a consumer with API key
3. Send request with:
   a) Valid key in header
   b) Invalid key
   c) No key
Expected results:
Valid key -> 200
Invalid key -> 401
No key -> 401

Title: JWT token validation
Description: Verify Kong validates JWT for Open Banking use case
Test steps:
1. Enable jwt plugin on a test route
2. Generate a valid JWT (RS256)
3. Send requests with:
   a) Valid JWT
   b) Expired JWT
   c) Tampered signature
   d) Wrong issuer
Expected results:
Valid JWT -> 200
All invalid variants -> 401/403

Title: mTLS client certificate
Description: Verify mTLS for partner/client certificate auth
Test steps:
1. Enable mTLS on a route
2. Generate test client cert (CA-signed)
3. Send request with:
   a) Valid client cert
   b) Expired cert
   c) No cert
Expected results:
Valid cert -> 200
Invalid/no cert -> 401

Title: IP whitelist/blacklist
Description: Verify IP restriction on routes
Test steps:
1. Configure IP restriction plugin (allow specific CIDR)
2. Send from allowed IP
3. Send from blocked IP
Expected results:
Allowed IP -> 200
Blocked IP -> 403

Title: Rate limiting (per client)
Description: Verify per-client rate limiting
Test steps:
1. Enable rate-limiting plugin (5 req/min)
2. Send 7 requests as same consumer in 1 min
3. Wait 1 min, send again
Expected results:
First 5 -> 200
6th & 7th -> 429
After cooldown -> 200

Title: Rate limiting (per route)
Description: Verify per-route rate limit independent of client
Test steps:
1. Set route-level rate limit: 100 req/min
2. Multiple consumers hit same route
3. Verify total across all consumers capped at 100
Expected results: Aggregate requests > 100 -> 429

Title: Circuit breaker
Description: Verify Kong protects backend from cascading failure
Test steps:
1. Configure circuit breaker on upstream
2. Make demo server slow/unresponsive
3. Send multiple requests
4. Verify circuit opens after threshold
Expected results:
After N failures -> circuit opens
Subsequent requests -> 503 fast-fail
After cooldown -> half-open -> retry

Title: Request/response transformation
Description: Verify Kong transforms headers
Test steps:
1. Configure route with header transformation plugin
2. Add/remove/modify headers via plugin
3. Verify demo server receives modified headers
Expected results: Headers added/removed/changed as configured

Title: Access logging
Description: Verify Kong logs all proxy requests
Test steps:
1. Enable http-log plugin
2. Send several test requests
3. Check log contains: timestamp, IP, method, path, status, latency, consumer ID
Expected results: Complete request log for every request

Title: Prometheus metrics
Description: Verify Kong exposes metrics for monitoring
Test steps:
1. Enable prometheus plugin
2. Hit /metrics endpoint on CP or DP
3. Verify metrics: kong_http_requests_total, latency p50/p95/p99, connections
Expected results: Metrics endpoint returns Prometheus-format data

Title: Health check
Description: Verify Kong health check detects backend failure
Test steps:
1. Configure active health check on upstream
2. Verify demo server is marked healthy
3. Stop demo server
4. Wait for health check interval
Expected results:
Kong marks backend unhealthy
Kong stops routing to unhealthy target

Title: Path-based routing
Description: Verify Kong routes based on request path
Test steps:
1. Configure two services:
   /api/demo1 -> demo server endpoint A
   /api/demo2 -> demo server endpoint B
2. Send requests to each path
Expected results: Each path routes to correct backend endpoint

Title: Host-based routing
Description: Verify Kong routes based on Host header
Test steps:
1. Configure two services:
   api.fubon-test.com -> demo A
   portal.fubon-test.com -> demo B
2. Send requests with different Host headers
Expected results: Each hostname routes to correct backend

Title: Load balancing
Description: Verify Kong load balances across targets
Test steps:
1. Configure upstream with 2 demo server instances
2. Send 10 requests
3. Check distribution across targets
Expected results:
Requests distributed (round-robin)
If one target down -> all traffic to remaining

Title: CORS handling
Description: Verify Kong handles CORS preflight for web clients
Test steps:
1. Enable CORS plugin on a route
2. Send OPTIONS request from browser origin
3. Verify Access-Control-* headers
Expected results:
Correct CORS headers for allowed origins
No CORS for disallowed origins

Title: Declarative config (decK)
Description: Verify Kong works with declarative YAML config
Test steps:
1. Export Kong config to YAML via decK
2. Modify config (add route)
3. Import config back via decK
4. Verify new route works
Expected results:
Export -> modify -> import cycle works
Routes functional after each step

Title: Config validation
Description: Verify Kong validates config before applying
Test steps:
1. Use deck validate on valid config
2. Intentionally break config (bad syntax)
3. Run validation again
Expected results:
Valid config -> passes
Broken config -> rejected with error details

Title: Zero-downtime reload
Description: Verify Kong reload without dropping requests
Test steps:
1. Start continuous requests to DP
2. Apply config change (add new route)
3. Check if any requests dropped
Expected results: Config changes applied without connection drops

Title: Kong latency overhead
Description: Measure Kong added latency
Test steps:
1. Ping demo server directly (no Kong) baseline
2. Send same request via Kong DP
3. Calculate difference
Expected results: Kong overhead < 5ms (p99)

Title: Throughput test
Description: Verify Kong handles expected TPS
Test steps:
1. Send parallel requests via Postman
2. Ramp up from low to target (e.g. 500 TPS)
3. Monitor Kong CPU/memory on K8s
Expected results:
No errors at expected TPS
CPU < 70%, memory stable
